title: How Cardano Governance Works
description: The big picture: who participates, how decisions are made, and what can be changed.

title: Who Votes on What
description: Deep dive into voting mechanics: which bodies vote on which actions, what the thresholds are, and why they differ.

title: Life of a Governance Action
description: Follow a proposal from submission to enactment: voting, ratification, chaining, and what can go wrong.

title: The Constitution and Its Enforcement
description: The written constitution, the committee that interprets it, and the Plutus script that enforces it.

title: How Smart Contract Costs Are Set
description: From Plutus benchmarks to on-chain parameters: how the price of running a smart contract gets decided.

title: EUTxO Fundamentals
description: The transaction model that makes Cardano smart contracts work.

title: Smart Contract Languages
description: Tour the on-chain languages targeting Plutus Core.

title: Vasil-Era Features
description: Reference scripts, reference inputs, and inline datums.

title: Smart Contract Patterns
description: State machines, oracles, staking validators, and minting policies.

title: Ecosystem Protocols
description: DEXes, lending, L2 scaling, and developer tools.

title: Wallet & Metadata Standards
description: CIP-30, CIP-95, CIP-100, CIP-108, CIP-119, CIP-88 — connecting wallets to governance and giving tokens identity.

title: Plutus V3 Upgrades
description: CIP-69, CIP-85, CIP-381 — smaller scripts, unified interfaces, and on-chain cryptography.

title: Hydra: Cardano's Layer 2
description: What Hydra is, how it scales Cardano, and why isomorphic state channels matter.

title: Life of a Hydra Head
description: Follow a head from initialization through fan-out: the five phases, the on-chain state machine, and what happens when things go wrong.
